text.title: Meta Ads Creative Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ads-meta-creative methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: First 3 seconds of every video creative MUST contain the hook (problem statement or pattern-interrupt). Brand logo or splash in seconds 0-3 is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Thumb-stop rate in seconds 0-3 is the single biggest predictor of CPM and CTR on Meta.

rule.statement: All creative MUST ship in 1:1, 4:5, or 9:16. Landscape (16:9) is forbidden for feed placements.
rule.rationale: Landscape crops eat 40% of the visible area on mobile feeds; CPM penalty + low CTR.

rule.statement: On-screen value proposition MUST be ≤8 words. Anything longer fails the 1-second scan.
rule.rationale: Users scan feed at 1.7 sec/post; >8 words = unreadable on mobile.

rule.statement: Each ad set MUST launch with 3-5 creative variants (different hooks, same value-prop) and rotate the bottom performer weekly.
rule.rationale: Single-creative ad sets fatigue within 5-7 days; matrix testing extends life 3-4x.

rule.statement: CTA MUST start with a verb (Get / See / Start / Try / Save). Noun-led CTAs ('Free demo', 'Pricing page') are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Verb-led CTAs lift CTR 15-30% on Meta in A/B tests.
